essence: David sets the day's direction: work through the API surface (ASP) and build Mochaccino screens around it, with every screen a direct reflection of real working code in the application.

actions.text: Build screens around the API surface that directly reflect real working code

essence: David directs that Mochaccino designs must bind to tangible programmable assets — the agent should push back when data is missing and resolve it via either a feature spec/ticket or schema-generated mock data — and asks for the channels/hosts/extensions/permissions list to be reorganized into a logical order (the v4 layout read better than v9).

actions.text: Reorganize the channels/hosts/extensions/permissions list into a logical, readable order
When designs need missing data, either raise a feature spec or generate schema-based mock data instead of hallucinating

essence: Meetup social catch-up where David reveals the NDIS SupportSignal partnership dissolved at the start of the month after one partner became belligerent, he now builds KyberAgent and the Kyber brain with Ian and Martin toward a dark-factory ticket autopilot on cheap/free models, wants YouTube back as his stability base, and shares Vietnam trip plans (dad and Barbara joining) plus Joy's new 24-hour nail/dress shop not yet profitable.

Chiang Mai AI meetup deep-dive on agent memory and context: message-envelope normalization for provider swapping, lossless-claw-style just-in-time compaction that preserves prior chunks, and David presenting KyberAgent's brain (RAG + entity graph + timeline + sleep-time memory built by Ian, tuned with RRF/recency re-ranking) with Lexi as the librarian keeping brains tagged.

Martin's Chiang Mai AI meetup talk decomposes agents into five primitives — message envelope, kernel/loop, transport normalization, tool dispatch, context management — arguing products (Claude Code, Codex, Hermes, OpenClaw) differ mainly in the harness around the same loop, with KyberAgent as the worked example.
A team member records that David assigned them work on concepts for a new social intro video to replace the current one on the website, planning to develop ideas and show him soon.

actions.text: Develop concepts for a new social intro video for the website and present to David

essence: David wants Jan and Mary onboarded to KyberAgent at the next meeting, and extends the recipe concept to whole applications: recipes that reverse-engineer, recreate, and customize apps built on his foundation stacks (AppyStack), packaged as sellable PDFs with the reference application trackable for pulling in upstream capabilities.

actions.text: Get Jan and Mary using KyberAgent starting at the next team meeting

essence: David sketches the skill-recipe distribution model: sell skills as specific problem-solvers, pair each with a recipe that can rebuild (or improve/customize) the skill, package prettily with a PDF, and distribute behind paywalls — $5 one-off website download or $5/month membership — across two communities (AppyDave and AI-TLDR), plus a meta-skill that generates recipes from existing skills.

actions.text: Build a skill that generates a recipe from an existing skill
Set up paywalled packaging (PDF + zip + community) for skill distribution

essence: David signs off warmly with Jan, glad he could spend time with family during a harrowing period, offering to connect tomorrow and asking him to pass wishes to his father.
David is frustrated that the system audit was not thorough — issues only surfaced after working the system again — leaving him unsure whether two or three overlapping mechanisms are still in place.

David directs a consolidation of sprawling audit skills (code-quality audit, system audit, delivery review — noting delivery review is used by app.supportsignal.com.au) into a routed system-audit concept, and wants the Extension SDK/host boundary split into packages with a real fake host (not mocks) so extensions get proper unit tests, plus a check whether the 'David Extension SDK overhaul' branch name is still relevant — all in long-horizon mode, not smallest-step mode.

actions.text: Investigate and consolidate overlapping audit/review skills into a routed system-audit with subtypes
Extract host interface into its own package and implement a fake host for proper extension unit testing
Check whether the Extension SDK overhaul branch name is still relevant

essence: David finds the extension channel/endpoint naming is arbitrary configuration rather than deterministic convention (Rails-style convention-over-configuration), demands a full architecture audit of the channels and data shapes between agent, extension SDK, brain, chat and host, wants one Mochaccino HTML page visualizing capabilities off real data, and flags that the Agent SDK is his purview but touching the host risks colliding with Ian and Martin's work.

actions.text: Run a full architecture audit of channel/endpoint naming and adopt convention over configuration
Create one HTML page visualizing capabilities driven off real system data

essence: David proposes scheduled tasks as a major Extension SDK capability: extensions declare cron-like schedules (configured in extension settings, exposed via data channels) but execution is owned by the host rather than each extension rolling its own scheduler — motivated by the OMI-fetch extension use case.

actions.text: Explore host-managed scheduled-task capability declared by extensions via data channels

essence: David proposes a watch-and-poll extension that monitors the OMI transcript/brain folder on a configurable interval (every 10 minutes or hourly) to detect changes and trigger actions, replacing his manual hunting through the brain folder.

actions.text: Build a watch-and-poll extension for OMI transcripts with configurable polling interval
